subject: English
title: Fear No More – Medium Short Notes

key_points: The poem 'Fear No More' is written by William Shakespeare and appears in the play Cymbeline
It presents death as a peaceful end to the struggles of life
The poem highlights that rich and poor people share the same fate after death
Natural hardships such as the heat of the sun and winter's rages symbolize life's difficulties
Death removes fear, suffering and social differences
characters: The deceased person – The person whose death is being mourned
Golden lads and girls – Symbol of wealthy and privileged people
Chimney sweepers – Symbol of poor workers
themes: Equality of all humans in death
Freedom from the hardships of life
literary_devices: Repetition – The phrase 'Fear no more' emphasizes the poem’s message
Imagery – Natural elements such as sun, winter, lightning and thunder
Contrast – Rich and poor people are compared to show equality in death
exam_tips: Mention that the poem is taken from the play Cymbeline
Explain how the poem shows equality between rich and poor
Focus on the comforting tone about death
